questions.prompt: Decision framework for where state should live?

choices.text: Ask in order: (1) only one component cares? → local (useState). (2) Sibling components share it? → lift to nearest common parent. (3) Deep subtree needs it? → composition (pass JSX as children) or context. (4) Whole app needs it? → external store (Zustand, Redux Toolkit). (5) Should persist across refresh/share? → URL. (6) From the server? → server-state library (TanStack Query, SWR). Most state ends at steps 1-2. Steps 4-6 are smaller categories than people assume. Default to local; promote only when actual sharing is needed.

questions.explanation: Local → lift → composition/context → store → URL → server library. Start small.

questions.prompt: Team is storing fetched API data (posts, users, products) in Redux. Manual loading flags, refetch logic, cache invalidation. Why this pattern struggles?

choices.text: Server state has different problems than client state: caching, deduplication, stale-while-revalidate, automatic refetching on focus, optimistic updates, request cancellation, retries. Redux (and Zustand, Jotai) are client-state tools — they store values but don't manage the data-fetching lifecycle. Teams end up reimplementing TanStack Query / SWR badly. Switch server data to a dedicated server-state library; Redux stays for genuine client state (theme, current user, app-wide settings). Clean separation: 'where does this data come from?' Server → query library. UI → client store. Reduces store size dramatically and fixes a lot of caching bugs.

questions.explanation: Server state needs server-state libraries (TanStack Query, SWR). Redux for client state only.
